1.title: Called into Fellowship
1.summary: Paul greets God’s sanctified church, gives thanks for grace already at work, and points to Christ’s faithfulness.

1.title: No Divisions in Christ
1.summary: Paul confronts rival loyalties and calls the church to unity around Christ rather than favored leaders.

1.title: The Wisdom of the Cross
1.summary: The message that appears foolish to the world reveals God’s saving power and overturns human standards of wisdom.

1.title: God Chooses the Lowly
1.summary: God works through the weak and overlooked so that every ground for boasting rests in Christ alone.

2.title: Christ Crucified, Not Eloquence
2.summary: Paul recalls proclaiming the cross in weakness so that Corinthian faith would rest on God’s power.

2.title: God’s Wisdom Revealed
2.summary: God’s hidden saving purpose surpasses worldly wisdom and is made known through the Spirit.

2.title: The Spirit and Christ’s Mind
2.summary: The Spirit enables believers to receive, understand, and speak the things freely given by God.

3.title: Signs of Spiritual Immaturity
3.summary: Jealousy and party loyalty reveal that the Corinthians are still thinking by ordinary human standards.

3.title: Servants in God’s Field
3.summary: Paul and Apollos perform different tasks, but God alone gives growth and owns the work.

3.title: Building on Christ Alone
3.summary: Christ is the church’s only foundation, and every worker’s contribution will be tested for lasting quality.

3.title: God’s Temple, God’s People
3.summary: Because the church belongs to God and is his holy temple, believers must reject boasting in human leaders.

4.title: Faithful Stewards of Christ
4.summary: Christian leaders are accountable to the Lord, who alone can expose motives and give final praise.

4.title: Pride Confronted by the Cross
4.summary: Paul contrasts Corinthian self-importance with the apostles’ humble, suffering, and Christ-shaped service.

4.title: A Father’s Warning and Appeal
4.summary: As their spiritual father, Paul urges imitation, sends Timothy, and prepares to confront empty arrogance.

5.title: Confronting Open Sin
5.summary: Paul commands the church to address blatant immorality for the offender’s ultimate good and the congregation’s holiness.

5.title: Remove the Old Leaven
5.summary: Because Christ is the church’s Passover sacrifice, its shared life must be marked by sincerity and truth.

5.title: Holiness Within the Church
5.summary: Paul distinguishes ordinary contact with the world from accountable fellowship with someone claiming faith while persisting in serious sin.

6.title: Lawsuits Before Unbelievers
6.summary: Believers should resolve disputes with godly wisdom rather than shame the church through hostile public litigation.

6.title: Washed and Made New
6.summary: Former patterns of sin no longer define those whom God has washed, sanctified, and justified.

6.title: The Body Belongs to Christ
6.summary: Christian freedom serves the Lord, and bodily union with Christ rules out union with sexual immorality.

6.title: Flee Sexual Immorality
6.summary: Believers must honor God with bodies purchased by Christ and inhabited by the Holy Spirit.

7.title: Mutual Faithfulness in Marriage
7.summary: Paul calls husbands and wives to mutual care, self-giving intimacy, prayer, and faithfulness according to God’s gifts.

7.title: Singleness, Marriage, and Separation
7.summary: Paul addresses unmarried believers, widows, married couples, and households divided by faith with pastoral realism.

7.title: Faithful in Your Calling
7.summary: Believers can obey God within varied social circumstances because belonging to Christ is their decisive identity.

7.title: Undivided Devotion in Troubled Times
7.summary: In light of present pressures, Paul explains how singleness may allow less distraction in serving the Lord.

7.title: Freedom to Marry or Remain Single
7.summary: Marriage and singleness can both honor God when chosen faithfully, wisely, and within devotion to the Lord.

8.title: Knowledge Must Serve Love
8.summary: Knowledge can inflate pride, but love builds others up and reflects a true relationship with God.

8.title: One God and One Lord
8.summary: Although idols have no true divine existence, believers confess one God and one Lord through whom all things exist.

8.title: Freedom That Protects Conscience
8.summary: Christian liberty must yield when its exercise could draw a vulnerable believer back toward idolatry.

9.title: An Apostle’s Right to Support
9.summary: Paul defends the legitimacy of material support for gospel workers through ordinary practice, Scripture, and Christ’s command.

9.title: Rights Surrendered for the Gospel
9.summary: Paul willingly refuses a legitimate benefit so that nothing will obstruct his proclamation of Christ.

9.title: Becoming a Servant to All
9.summary: Paul adapts his approach without compromising the gospel so that more people may hear and be saved.

9.title: Running with Discipline
9.summary: The Christian mission requires purposeful self-control like an athlete training to finish and receive the prize.

10.title: Privilege Does Not Prevent Failure
10.summary: Israel shared remarkable covenant blessings, yet many fell because outward privilege did not guarantee faithful obedience.

10.title: Warnings and a Way of Escape
10.summary: Israel’s failures warn believers against craving evil, while God’s faithfulness provides endurance under temptation.

10.title: Flee Idolatry
10.summary: Participation at the Lord’s table is incompatible with fellowship at an idolatrous table.

10.title: Freedom for Another’s Good
10.summary: Believers may receive God’s gifts gratefully while limiting freedom for the conscience and good of others.

10.title: Do All for God’s Glory
10.summary: Every choice should honor God and seek the salvation and well-being of other people.

11.title: Imitation and Honor in Worship
11.summary: Paul calls believers to follow Christ-shaped example and preserve fitting honor as men and women pray and prophesy.

11.title: Divisions at the Lord’s Table
11.summary: Social selfishness and humiliation of poorer believers contradict the shared meal’s meaning.

11.title: Remembering and Proclaiming Christ
11.summary: The bread and cup remember the Lord’s sacrificial death and proclaim it until he comes.

11.title: Discern the Body Together
11.summary: Believers must examine themselves, recognize the gathered body, and receive the meal with patient mutual care.

12.title: Different Gifts, One Spirit
12.summary: The Holy Spirit distributes varied gifts for the common good while uniting every true confession around Jesus as Lord.

12.title: Many Members, One Body
12.summary: Baptism joins diverse believers into one body in which every member has a God-appointed place.

12.title: Every Member Needs the Others
12.summary: No member is self-sufficient, and God calls the whole body to shared honor, care, suffering, and joy.

12.title: God Appoints Gifts for Service
12.summary: God establishes different ministries within the church while directing believers toward a still greater way.

13.title: Nothing Without Love
13.summary: Speech, knowledge, faith, sacrifice, and generosity lose their spiritual worth when separated from love.

13.title: The Character of Love
13.summary: Love expresses itself through patience, humility, truthfulness, endurance, trust, and hope.

13.title: Love Never Fails
13.summary: Present gifts and partial understanding will pass away, but faith, hope, and especially love endure.

14.title: Gifts That Build Up
14.summary: Paul prioritizes intelligible prophecy over uninterpreted tongues because gathered worship should strengthen the church.

14.title: Speak So Others Understand
14.summary: Spiritual speech benefits others only when it communicates meaning, so worship should engage both spirit and understanding.

14.title: Mature Worship Before Outsiders
14.summary: Orderly, intelligible prophecy can expose the heart and lead an unbelieving visitor to acknowledge God’s presence.

14.title: Orderly Participation for Edification
14.summary: Songs, teachings, revelations, tongues, and prophecies should be shared in turn under self-control for the church’s good.

14.title: Decency and Order in Worship
14.summary: Paul closes with instructions for submission, discernment, eagerness for prophecy, and orderly practice.

15.title: The Gospel of the Risen Christ
15.summary: Paul restates the received gospel, names witnesses to Christ’s resurrection, and celebrates grace at work in his ministry.

15.title: Why Resurrection Is Essential
15.summary: Without resurrection Christian faith collapses, but Christ has risen as the firstfruits and will defeat every enemy.

15.title: Live in Resurrection Hope
15.summary: Christian sacrifice and holy living make sense because death is not the end and resurrection is certain.

15.title: The Resurrection Body
15.summary: God will raise a transformed, glorious, Spirit-empowered body fitted to bear the image of the heavenly Christ.

15.title: Death Swallowed Up in Victory
15.summary: At Christ’s coming mortality will become immortality, death will be defeated, and faithful labor will prove worthwhile.

16.title: The Collection for Jerusalem
16.summary: Paul gives practical instructions for regular, accountable generosity toward believers in need.

16.title: Plans for Paul, Timothy, and Apollos
16.summary: Paul explains changing travel plans and urges the Corinthians to welcome fellow workers without rivalry.

16.title: Stand Firm and Honor Faithful Workers
16.summary: The church must combine courage and love while recognizing servants who refresh and strengthen God’s people.

16.title: Final Greetings and Grace
16.summary: Paul closes with greetings, a call to love Christ, and blessings of grace and affection.
